ai_summary: Bajaj Auto Limited reported strong Q1 FY27 consolidated financial performance, with revenue surging 65% and profit attributable to owners increasing 46% year-over-year, driven by robust volume growth. Despite this, net profit declined 12% sequentially, largely due to a negative swing in the share of associate profits. A material ₹5,632.80 crore share buyback was completed post-quarter end, set to reduce outstanding shares and boost future EPS, though the company notes an inability to reliably estimate new environmental (EPR) obligations, presenting a future financial uncertainty.
